title: Player Creation — Input
description: Expand the game to create a player character. Use getline to read the player's name (which may contain spaces). Declare int variables for health, gold, and attackPower with initial values. Use cin to let the player choose a starting class (1=Warrior, 2=Mage, 3=Rogue) which sets different stat values. Display the player's final stats. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert '#include <string>' in cpp, 'Must include <string> for string type'
assert 'getline' in cpp, 'Use getline to read the player name (handles spaces)'
assert 'cin' in cpp, 'Use cin to read the player class choice'
assert 'int health' in cpp or 'int health;' in cpp or 'health =' in cpp, 'Declare an int variable for health'
assert 'int gold' in cpp or 'int gold;' in cpp or 'gold =' in cpp, 'Declare an int variable for gold'
assert 'int attackPower' in cpp or 'int attack' in cpp or 'int damage' in cpp, 'Declare an int variable for attack power'
assert 'cout' in cpp, 'Use cout to display player stats'
acceptance_criteria: Uses getline to read the player name
Uses cin to read a numeric class choice
Declares int variables for health, gold, and attack power
Different class choices set different stat values
Displays the final player stats using cout
hints: Use getline(cin, playerName) to read the full name including spaces
After cin >> choice, add cin.ignore() before getline to clear the newline from the buffer
Warrior might have 100 health, 50 gold, 15 attack; Mage might have 70 health, 80 gold, 20 attack
Don't forget to include <string> for the string type
